quote: 1. It's far better to buy a wonderful company at a fair price than a fair company at a wonderful price.
speaker: Warren Buffett

quote: 2. Being too far ahead of your time is indistinguishable from being wrong.
speaker: Howard Marks

quote: 3. Buy stocks, take sleeping pills, and look at the prices no more.
speaker: André Kostolany

quote: 4. I'm only rich because I know when I'm wrong.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 5. It's not whether you're right or wrong, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 6. Price is what you pay; value is what you get.
speaker: Warren Buffett

quote: 7. You can't predict. You can prepare.
speaker: Howard Marks

quote: 8. Whoever doesn't have patience shouldn't bother with stocks.
speaker: André Kostolany

quote: 9. Invert, always invert.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 10. Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful.
11. Experience is what you got when you didn't get what you wanted.
12. The big money is not in the buying and selling, but in the waiting.
13. Spend each day trying to be a little wiser than you were when you woke up.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 14. If we avoid the losers, the winners will take care of themselves.
speaker: Howard Marks

quote: 15. Knowing what you don't know is more useful than being brilliant.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 16. The first rule of compounding: never interrupt it unnecessarily.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 17. Markets are constantly in a state of uncertainty and flux, and money is made by discounting the obvious and betting on the unexpected.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 18. The amateur thinks about how much money he can make. The professional thinks about how much money he can lose.
speaker: André Kostolany

quote: 19. Our favorite holding period is forever.
speaker: Warren Buffett

quote: 20. The markets are practically always wrong, but practically always there is a prevailing bias that can be detected.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 21. There are old investors and there are bold investors, but there are no old, bold investors.
speaker: André Kostolany

quote: 22. It is remarkable how much long-term advantage people like us have gotten by trying to be consistently not stupid, instead of trying to be very intelligent.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 23. All I want to know is where I'm going to die, so I'll never go there.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 24. Money is made in the boredom phase, lost in the excitement phase.
speaker: André Kostolany

quote: 25. Acknowledging what you don't know is the dawning of wisdom.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 26. Reflexivity is a two-way feedback mechanism in which reality helps shape the participants' thinking and the participants' thinking helps shape reality.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 27. A man can choose different ways to get rid of his fortune: the fastest is the casino, the most pleasurable is with beautiful women and the most stupid is at the stock exchange.
speaker: André Kostolany

quote: 28. Risk comes from not knowing what you are doing.
speaker: Warren Buffett

quote: 29. A great business at a fair price is superior to a fair business at a great price.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 30. Investment success doesn't come from "buying good things," but rather from "buying things well."
speaker: Howard Marks

quote: 31. Show me the incentive and I will show you the outcome.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 32. Once we realize the imperfection of all human thought, it is not shocking any more to discover that our theories, our constructs, are imperfect.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 33. There's no investment idea so good that it can't be ruined by paying too much for it.
speaker: Howard Marks

quote: 34. Never invest in a business you cannot understand.
speaker: Warren Buffett

quote: 35. Anyone who thinks financial markets can be captured in mathematical equations is deluding himself.
speaker: George Soros

quote: 36. Superior investors think about risk before they think about return.
speaker: Howard Marks

quote: 37. The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
speaker: Warren Buffett

quote: 38. It is not a bad idea to be a little wary of good ideas.
speaker: Charlie Munger

quote: 39. Rule number one: most things are cyclical.
speaker: Howard Marks
